$comment: Layer B selection cases for BATCHSUM vs the real GnuCOBOL harness image (record protocol: the case's stdin lines ARE the input file's records via the Dockerfile.infile wrapper). A candidate with a discarded priming read loses the first record from COUNT and TOTAL on every non-empty file; the empty file agrees by accident, which is why it is mandatory but not sufficient. Cases cover the empty file (AT END on the first READ), a single record, a typical batch, and a truncating record value.

legacy.label: COBOL 85 BATCHSUM (GnuCOBOL via harness image legacymind/legacy-batchsum, record-stream wrapper)
